channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar
item.description: A ClaudeBleed-linked vulnerability reportedly persists across eight patches, exposing potentially sensitive data to other extensions. The post Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Gateway List updates using CSV not applied
item.description: Jul 14, 12:52 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 14, 12:41 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 14, 11:55 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue where changes applied via CSV to Gateway Lists are not being...

item.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the modern firmware standard. "An attacker exploiting one of these vulnerable applications can execute...

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window...

item.title: SAP Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in NetWeaver, Approuter, Commerce Cloud
item.description: The flaws could allow attackers to access and modify data, and cause system unavailability and request-response desynchronization. The post SAP Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in NetWeaver, Approuter, Commerce Cloud appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 14, 09:56 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 14, 09:51 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Codespaces has been mitigated....

item.title: Valarian Raises $50 Million for Sovereign Infrastructure Control Layer
item.description: UK-based cybersecurity firm Valarian has raised a total of $70 million for its ACRA technology. The post Valarian Raises $50 Million for Sovereign Infrastructure Control Layer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Multiple Jscrambler Packages Impacted by Supply Chain Attack
item.description: A threat actor poisoned several Jscrambler NPM package versions to drop a cross-platform credential stealer. The post Multiple Jscrambler Packages Impacted by Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Grok Build Uploads Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Reads
item.description: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. A researcher publishing as cereblab, testing version 0.2.93, captured one of those uploads, cloned the git bundle out...

item.title: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans. The VPN, named First VPN Service (1VPNS),...

item.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
item.description: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute...

item.title: Why APEC 2026 Spotlight Turns to Chengdu: A Western Chinese City Digital Economy Transformation
item.description: Chengdu hosts APEC Digital and AI Ministerial Meeting from July 16-29, showcasing a systematic digital economy infrastructure from 29,000P computing capacity to 53.96 billion data records open.

item.title: Tao Law V2 Delivers Measured Results: Huawei Kirin 2026 Chip Shows 53.5% Transistor Density Leap as Semiconductor Rally Broadens
item.description: Huawei Tao Law V2 publishes Kirin 2026 measured data showing 238MTr/mm² density via LogicFolding while Goldman Sachs reweights toward semiconductors, signaling a full-industry-cycle rally.

item.title: Quantum Heavyweight Arrives: Zhongqi Wuliang to Debut Data-Center-Grade Quantum Computer at WAIC
item.description: Shanghai-based neutral atom quantum computing startup Zhongqi Wuliang, spun out from CAS SIOM, to unveil the world first data-center-deployable quantum computer concept at WAIC 2026.

item.title: JD.com Enters Embodied AI: First RoboBase Robot Facility Breaks Ground in Guangzhou
item.description: JD.com invests $1.4B in nationwide RoboBase network as it transforms from logistics giant to embodied AI platform, planning 300 million robot procurements and 80+ bases across China.

item.title: 478x Faster Than NVIDIA A100: Peking University and CAS Neuromorphic Chip Published in Science
item.description: A 40nm phase-change memristor neural dynamics chip achieves 2.12ms computation latency and outperforms NVIDIA A100 GPU by 50-478x in brain cortex reconstruction, published in Science.

item.title: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
item.description: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog. The packages did not go after the developers who might install them. The operators used the...

item.title: Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase 2 as It Rethinks Contractor Cybersecurity Rules
item.description: A new CMMC review and reform task force will conduct a comprehensive review of the program. The post Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase 2 as It Rethinks Contractor Cybersecurity Rules appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Haikou, China (HAK)
item.description: Jul 14, 06:30 UTC Resolved - Earlier today Cloudflare experienced network degradation in Haikou, China that affected some users. The period of impact was roughly between 14th July 2026, 05:45 - 06:07 UTC. Those issues have been resolved.

item.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
item.description: Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform. The way in has been the trust the organization had already extended, usually through the...

item.title: Elevated R2 errors in EU region
item.description: Jul 14, 04:36 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 14, 04:00 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 14, 03:46 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is observing issues with Cloudflare R2 service.We are currently working on a resolution. More...

item.title: Elevated errors in item PUT requests to AI Search
item.description: Jul 14, 04:01 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is observing issues with Cloudflare AI Search service.We are currently working on a resolution. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: From ML Predictions to Informed Diagnostic Assistance Using the Toulmin Model of Argumentation
item.description: arXiv:2607.09664v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To provide a structured and interpretable assessment, we decompose the image-based diagnosis into components following the Toulmin model of argumentation. This model consists of a claim, grounds, warrant, qualifier, rebuttal, and backing. Consider a...

item.title: Format Sensitivity Index: Token-Controlled Prompt Wrapper Robustness and Schema Compliance in LLM Benchmarking
item.description: arXiv:2607.09665v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prompt wrappers often differ only in formatting, yet they can change model scores enough to flip leaderboard conclusions. We study this variance under a token-controlled protocol and introduce two complementary metrics: the Format Sensitivity Index...

item.title: Faithful, Not Corrective: Message-Format Effects in Multi-Hop Agent Relays Are Tier-Dependent
item.description: arXiv:2607.09678v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When LLM agents hand off information to one another, does the message format matter? Two literatures disagree: format-optimization work reports that structured messages cut cost without hurting accuracy, while format-restriction work finds that...

item.title: Boltzmann MapReduce: A Partition-Function Reduce for Forkable Sandboxes
item.description: arXiv:2607.09689v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To leading order under local asymptotic normality (LAN), the confidence density a worker emits over a chunk of size $n$ is a Gibbs--Boltzmann measure $\exp\{-\beta E(\theta)\}$ whose inverse temperature is the sample size, $\beta=n$. Three...

item.title: Interpreting Latent CoT Reasoning as Dynamical Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.09698v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent latent reasoning methods, such as CODI and COCONUT, face a fundamental interpretability problem: they maintain multiple superimposed candidate traces in the hidden space at each step, unlike explicit- CoT, which follows a single transparent...

item.title: Knowledge Graphs Meet Graph Neural Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
item.description: arXiv:2607.09666v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have emerged as a powerful paradigm in Knowledge Graphs (KGs) due to their intrinsic ability to model graph-structured data. However, there remains a lack of a systematic review about GNN-based methodologies across the...

item.title: Position: Every Ground Truth is a Human Construction, not an Objective Truth
item.description: arXiv:2607.09668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ground truth datasets play a fundamental role as reference values in the training and evaluation of machine learning models. This position paper argues that ground truths are not neutral objective measurements that are naturally given, but instead...

item.title: AuditWeave: A Tamper-Evident, Auditor-Navigable Evidence Layer for AI-Assisted and Data-Transformation Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2607.09682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI systems are increasingly used to assist consequential decisions in regulated domains such as auditing, finance, and healthcare. This creates a recurring obligation: an organization must be able to reconstruct, after the fact, which evidence...

item.title: Ablation, Statistical Inference, and Validation for KV-Cache Compression
item.description: arXiv:2607.09683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study systematically compares Turbo-Quant and SpectralQuant KV-cache compression, evaluating non-dominated schemes, including WHT rotation with Beta Lloyd-Max and QJL, through a statistical validation methodology that separates systematic codec...

item.title: SciML in the Wild: A Diagnostic Study of When Structural Priors Help and When They Hurt
item.description: arXiv:2607.09684v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) methods such as Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs), Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs), and Universal Differential Equations (UDEs) are most effective when structural priors reflect reliable...

item.title: CLIR-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Question Answering over Irregular Clinical Time Series
item.description: arXiv:2607.09880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical time series are central to patient monitoring, risk assessment, and clinical decision support. However, they are often sparse, irregularly sampled, and asynchronous, making it difficult for models to identify the temporal evidence required...

item.title: Index SLM Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2607.09885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Index-1.9B, a series of open small language models developed at Bilibili. The series comprises four models: Index-1.9B-Base, a foundation model with 1.9 billion non-embedding parameters pre-trained on 2.8 trillion predominantly Chinese and...

item.title: RouteRec: Strict Evaluation of Recommender-Agent Selection and Aggregation
item.description: arXiv:2607.09908v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recommender systems increasingly face a choice among heterogeneous agents -- collaborative filters, sequential models, content-based retrievers, and LLM-based rerankers -- yet no single agent is uniformly best. We study this choice as task-aware agent...

item.title: Global Merger-Arbitrage Forecasting with Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.09921v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a language-model forecasting system for merger arbitrage, a specialized high-stakes financial setting in which the task is to predict the outcome of announced M\&A deals. Unlike prior work on judgmental forecasting with LLMs, which has...

item.title: Faithful by Design: Evaluating and Improving LLM-Generated Clinical Trial Summaries for Multi-Stakeholder Audiences
item.description: arXiv:2607.09932v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used to summarize clinical trial results for healthcare providers, patients, and payers, but their tendency to hallucinate poses significant risks in this high-stakes context. This study introduces a benchmark...

item.title: [AINews] Codex usage up >10x in 6 months to 7M users, +1M in the past ~day; did Codex overtake Claude Code??
item.description: a quiet day lets us fact check some numbers against the sound of silence of Claude Code reporting...

item.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s...

item.title: Incorrect zone limits impacting some customers
item.description: Jul 13, 16:52 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 13, 16:36 UTC Investigating - Some customers may see "You have exceeded the limit for adding zones" messages when there are within the limits. A fix has been identified and is in progress

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 13, 15:24 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 13, 15:04 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 13, 14:44 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity....

item.title: Reproducing human biases in route choice using large language models: Toward scalable behavioral modeling
item.description: Human choice behavior, including route choice, exhibits systematic behavioral biases that deviate from the assumptions of full rationality. Cumulative prospect theory (CPT) has been widely recognized as an effective framework for characterizing such behavioral patterns. However, its large-scale...

item.title: Actions runs are experiencing failures to start
item.description: Jul 13, 13:53 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 13, 13:39 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Actions and Pages has been...

item.title: Elevated errors when creating sites in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Sites (Operational)

item.title: Comparative Analysis of GAT and BERT for Human-Like Playtesting
item.description: Accurately modeling and understanding player experience is crucial for designing engaging puzzle games. To achieve this, a common approach involves collecting diverse user data to train predictive playtesting models that mimic player behavior. However, existing data-driven methods often lack the...

item.title: Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
item.description: Google and AIM launched ATL Saathi, a Gemini-powered AI tool empowering Indian educators in robotics labs.

item.title: HyperSafe: Inference-Time Safety Recovery for Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: Safety alignment in large language models can be fragile under fine-tuning, as even benign task adaptation may increase harmful compliance. Existing defenses mainly follow two directions: they either intervene during or after fine-tuning through retraining or weight modification, which can be...

item.title: The Download: a donor conception cap and world models for AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a...

item.title: UK and Allies urge critical sectors to improve defences against Russian intelligence targeting
item.description: New advisory highlights Russian state cyber actors’ global exploitation of poorly configured routers
